target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

target.title: fix(browser): notify agent when click triggers download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a browser `/act` action triggers a file download, OpenClaw already saves the unmanaged Playwright download, but the agent-visible `/act` response did not include the downloaded filename or saved path.

That meant an agent could successfully trigger a download, receive only a generic success response, and then retry because it had no completion/path feedback for the saved file.

Fixes #93250

## Why This Change Was Made

This change adds action-scoped download capture around Playwright-backed `/act` execution and returns optional `downloads` metadata in the `/act` JSON response.

The response includes the saved path and suggested filename for downloads observed during click, coordinate click, batch, and evaluate actions. Overlapping actions on the same page intentionally receive page-level download metadata so a real browser download is not silently dropped when `/act` calls overlap.

## User Impact

Agents can now see that a browser-triggered download completed, including where the file was saved, and avoid retrying an action that already produced the expected file.

Existing `/act` responses remain compatible: the `downloads` field is optional and only appears when a download is captured.

## Evidence

Live click-triggered `/act` proof on macOS, Node.js v26.0.0, Chrome 149.0.7827.197:

```bash
$ curl --noproxy '*' -s -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:53558/
HTTP 200

$ curl --noproxy '*' -s http://127.0.0.1:53559/json/version | node -e "..."
Chrome/149.0.7827.197

$ node click-act-proof.mts
POST /act { kind: 'click', selector: '#download' }

POST /act RESPONSE (agent-visible JSON)
{
  "downloads": {
    "count": 1,
    "recent": [
      {
        "suggestedFilename": "act-click-proof-download.txt",
        "savedPath": "/tmp/openclaw/downloads/8a8d3c2f-3b69-4b8f-aa74-ad75f8d441e3-act-click-proof-download.txt"
      }
    ]
  }
}

$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const p='/tmp/openclaw/downloads/8a8d3c2f-3b69-4b8f-aa74-ad75f8d441e3-act-click-proof-download.txt'; console.log('exists:', fs.existsSync(p), 'content:', fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8').trim())"
exists: true content: OpenClaw /act click download proof - PR 93307
```

Focused tests:

```bash
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-browser.config.ts browser/src/browser/pw-session.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  21 passed (21)
```

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-browser.config.ts browser/src/browser/server.agent-contract-core.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
```

target.title: [codex] Add plugin task lifecycle API
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can inspect task runs today, but they do not have a narrow public SDK path for creating a plugin-owned task run, reporting progress, and finalizing the run while preserving owner/session scoping. That leaves plugin task lifecycle behavior either unavailable or tied to internal core task APIs that are not safe for third-party plugin contracts.

This PR adds a plugin-safe task run lifecycle API under `api.runtime.tasks.runs.bindSession(...).lifecycle` / `fromToolContext(...).lifecycle`. Plugin-created records are stored as existing `cli` task runtime rows with a required plugin-namespaced `taskKind`, and task creation identity now includes `taskKind` so same-owner/same-run records with different plugin task kinds stay separated.

## Summary

- Adds a plugin-safe task run lifecycle API under `api.runtime.tasks.runs.bindSession(...).lifecycle` / `fromToolContext(...).lifecycle`.
- Creates plugin-owned records as existing `cli` task runtime rows with required plugin-namespaced `taskKind`.
- Adds owner-scoped exact create/progress/finalize behavior and exposes `taskKind` in task run DTOs.
- Documents plugin-owned task records and SDK lifecycle usage.

## Evidence

Focused validation:

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  71 passed (71)
```

$ pnpm tsx --eval '<runtime lifecycle create/progress/finalize proof script>'
{

    "runtime": "cli",
    "sessionKey": "agent:proof:telegram:group:redacted",
    "ownerKey": "agent:proof:telegram:group:redacted",

target.title: feat(web-fetch): add Tavily as a native `web_fetch` provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Tavily ships as a `web_search` provider and exposes `tavily_extract` as a tool, but `tools.web.fetch` only auto-detects Firecrawl. Users with a Tavily key have no first-class `web_fetch` path.
- Why it matters: parity with the Tavily `web_search` story and with Firecrawl's two-key model. One Tavily account covers `/search` and `/extract`, so onboarding shouldn't ask for a second key.
- What changed: register `createTavilyWebFetchProvider` (auto-detect order `70`, after Firecrawl at `50`) backed by `runTavilyExtract`; declare `contracts.webFetchProviders: ["tavily"]` and add `webFetch.apiKey`/`webFetch.baseUrl` config; resolve credentials from `webFetch.apiKey` → `webSearch.apiKey` → `TAVILY_API_KEY`; 
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no Tavily-only knobs added to `web_fetch` (`query`, `chunks_per_source`, `include_images`, `include_favicon`, `timeout`, `include_usage`, multi-URL stay on `tavily_extract`); no changes to the Firecrawl provider, the `tavily_search`/`tavily_extract` tools, or the `web_fetch` contract/SDK shape.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New bundled `web_fetch` provider id `tavily`, selectable via `tools.web.fetch.provider: "tavily"` or auto-detected when only `TAVILY_API_KEY` (or `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`) is set. Firecrawl wins auto-detect when both are configured (order 50 vs 70).
- New optional config: `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webFetch.apiKey` (override) and `webFetch.baseUrl`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A (provider plumbing only)
- Integration/channel: Tavily API (`api.tavily.com`)
- Relevant config (redacted):
```json5
{
   "plugins":{
      "entries":{
         "tavily":{
            "enabled":true,
            "config":{
               "webSearch":{
                  "apiKey":"tvly-***"
               }
            }
         }
      }
   },
   "tools":{
      "web":{
         "fetch":{
            "provider":"tavily"
         }
      }
   }
}
```

### Steps

1. Set `TAVILY_API_KEY` (or `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`).
2. Configure `tools.web.fetch.provider: "tavily"`.
3. Invoke `web_fetch` with a URL

### Expected

- Tavily provider is selected; `/extract` is called once.
- Result honors `maxChars` truncation on `rawContent` and `content`.

### Actual

- Matches expected. With both Firecrawl and Tavily keys set, auto-detect picks Firecrawl first (50 < 70).

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets:
  - `pnpm test extensions/tavily` → `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 16 passed (16)`.
  - `pnpm check:changed` → green; lanes: changelog attributions, wildcard re-export checks, dup coverage, `tsgo:extensions`, `tsgo:extensions:test`, `lint:extensions` (0 warnings/errors), runtime sidecar loader guard, runtime import cycles (`0 runtime value cycle(s)`). Exit 0.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` → clean.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm test extensions/tavily` and `pnpm check:changed` locally; manually walked through `index.ts:11` registration order, manifest `contracts.webFetchProviders` + `configSchema.properties.webFetch`, `resolveTavilyApiKey` precedence, and `applySelectionConfig` enabling the plugin.
- Edge cases checked: `webFetch.apiKey` override beats `webSearch.apiKey`; `extractMode: "text"` flips `format`; `maxChars` truncates both `rawContent` and `content`; passing no `maxChars` returns the payload untouched; non-string content is left alone.
- What I did **not** verify: N/A

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

None

target.title: fix(daemon): load <stateDir>/gateway.env into runtime env
target.body:
## Summary

`loadGlobalRuntimeDotEnvFiles` was reading `<stateDir>/.env` and (as a fallback) `~/.config/openclaw/gateway.env`, but never `<stateDir>/gateway.env`. That left a real-world gap: many users keep API keys and `GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN` in `~/.openclaw/gateway.env` because the on-disk service-env wrapper file lives next to it. When `openclaw doctor --fix` regenerates `service-env/<label>.env`, it pulls secret refs (e.g. `gateway.auth.token = ${GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN}`) from `process.env`. With `~/.openclaw/gateway.env` never sourced, the key is missing in `process.env`, the regenerated service-env drops it, and the LaunchAgent crash-loops with `SecretRefResolutionError`.

This PR adds `<stateDir>/gateway.env` to the dotenv loader chain, alongside the existing `<stateDir>/.env` and the XDG `~/.config/openclaw/gateway.env` compat path. Conflict-resolution semantics keep `<stateDir>/.env` winning for shared keys.

Reproduction:
1. Put `GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN=...` in `~/.openclaw/gateway.env` only (not in shell).
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix`.
3. Observe `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` — repeated `SecretRefResolutionError: Environment variable "GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN" is missing or empty` entries.

After this PR the regenerated service-env keeps the user key.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/infra/dotenv.test.ts` — 29 tests pass, including the new "loads ~/.openclaw/gateway.env so service-install commands inherit user-defined keys" case
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck and tests green; only failure is the unrelated pre-existing `doctor-state-integrity.ts:875` lint warning from commit 0f120c09ba
- [x] Manual: regenerated service-env locally and confirmed gateway came back up after the fix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Add WhatsApp DM message-tool reply mode
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: WhatsApp DMs used automatic final-reply delivery while groups used `message_tool_only`, so DMs could not reliably show live `message(action=send)` updates.
- Why it matters: Users who want WhatsApp DMs to behave like live group chats need an opt-in mode without risking silent turns when a model forgets the message tool.
- What changed: Added `channels.whatsapp.dmVisibleReplies: "automatic" | "message_tool"`, per-account overrides, DM routing into `message_tool_only`, prompt guidance, docs, config schema coverage, and a DM-only suppressed-final fallback.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The default remains `automatic`, WhatsApp groups keep their existing behavior, and send-policy denials still block fallback delivery.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #N/A
- Related #N/A
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: WhatsApp direct chats and groups intentionally used different source reply delivery modes, and direct chats had no supported opt-in for group-like message-tool delivery.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing coverage did not assert a DM-specific message-tool-only mode with a same-chat final fallback.
- Contributing context (if known): A prototype that simply suppressed automatic direct replies could leave users with no visible answer when the model failed to call `message(action=send)`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: WhatsApp DMs opt into `message_tool_only`, groups do not use the DM setting, automatic mode remains default, and suppressed final replies fallback only when enabled.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior crosses config resolution, WhatsApp inbound dispatch, and shared source-reply suppression, so targeted unit/seam tests cover the actual contract boundaries without a live WhatsApp session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

Adds an opt-in WhatsApp config key:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool",
    },
  },
}
```

`"automatic"` remains the default. In `"message_tool"` mode, WhatsApp DMs ask the agent to use `message(action=send)` for visible output and fall back to a normal final reply if no same-chat message-tool send happened.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
WhatsApp DM -> automatic final reply delivery

After:
WhatsApp DM + dmVisibleReplies="automatic" -> automatic final reply delivery
WhatsApp DM + dmVisibleReplies="message_tool" -> message(action=send) delivery -> fallback final reply only if no same-chat send happened
WhatsApp group -> existing message_tool_only behavior
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
- Model/provider: mocked test resolver
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.whatsapp.dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool"`

### Steps

1. Configure WhatsApp DM visible replies with `dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool"`.
2. Process a direct WhatsApp inbound message.
3. Verify shared dispatch receives `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`.
4. Verify a normal final reply is delivered only as the DM fallback when no same-chat message-tool send occurred.

### Expected

- DMs default to automatic final delivery.
- DMs opt into message-tool-only visible replies with a safety fallback.
- Groups remain unchanged.

### Actual

- Matches expected in targeted tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted local checks passed:

```bash
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched-ts-files>
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm config:docs:check
git diff --check
```

`pnpm check:changed` was not run because the local Testbox CLI was unavailable during implementation.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Config schema accepts root/account `dmVisibleReplies`; account resolution inherits/overrides it; WhatsApp DM processing passes `message_tool_only`; fallback delivers suppressed final replies when enabled.
- Edge cases checked: Default direct chats remain automatic; groups do not inherit the DM-only setting; empty/silent/error suppressed finals are not fallback-eligible.
- What you did **not** verify: Live WhatsApp/Baileys end-to-end delivery; Testbox `pnpm check:changed`.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Existing configs need no changes. To opt in, set `channels.whatsapp.dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool"` globally or under a WhatsApp account.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Models may still return final assistant text instead of using `message(action=send)`.
  - Mitigation: WhatsApp DMs in this mode keep a same-chat final-reply fallback, and the system prompt now explicitly says visible output requires `message(action=send)`.

target.title: fix(exec): decode Windows command output with codepage-aware streaming
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix Windows exec output mojibake for CJK locales by decoding captured command output with the active Windows code page instead of assuming UTF-8.
- Preserve decoder state across stdout/stderr chunks or decode buffered byte streams so split GBK/Shift_JIS characters are not replaced.
- Add focused regression coverage for CP936/GBK Chinese output and split multibyte chunks.

## Credit
This carries forward the useful approach from @slepybear's source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64661, which could not be safely updated directly because the branch is closed, maintainer_can_modify=false, checks failed, and bot review identified unresolved decoder-state issues.

## Linked Issues
Fixes/addresses the Windows CJK exec mojibake family reported in #40613, #50519, and #56462.

target.title: fix(signal): enable inbound status reactions
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: Signal has reaction send helpers, but inbound runs never wired `messages.statusReactions.enabled` into the shared status reaction lifecycle.
- Why it matters: Signal users get no queued/thinking/tool/compacting/done/error visual feedback during long-running agent turns even when status reactions are configured.
- What changed: Create a Signal status reaction controller for eligible inbound messages, route lifecycle callbacks through `dispatchInboundMessage`, and send reactions via `sendReactionSignal` for direct and group messages.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No config shape changes, no Signal REST/native transport changes, and no agent/tool message-action behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72020
- Related #20393
- Related #65919
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Signal's inbound handler dispatched messages without creating a `StatusReactionController` or passing lifecycle callbacks (`onReplyStart`, `onToolStart`, compaction callbacks, terminal state handling) into `dispatchInboundMessage`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Signal had send-reaction helper tests, but no inbound regression test for `messages.statusReactions.enabled`.
- Contributing context (if known): Other channels already wire the shared status reaction controller; Signal had the transport primitive but not the inbound lifecycle wiring.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.status-reactions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Signal direct and group inbound messages with `messages.statusReactions.enabled` send queued and done reactions through the correct direct recipient or groupId/targetAuthor route.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It exercises the Signal inbound handler, dispatch callback wiring, and Signal reaction transport seam without needing a live Signal daemon.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Signal now honors `messages.statusReactions.enabled` for inbound runs by updating reactions on the triggering message during the agent lifecycle.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Signal inbound -> dispatchInboundMessage -> no status reaction callbacks -> no lifecycle reactions

After:
Signal inbound -> StatusReactionController -> dispatch lifecycle callbacks -> sendReactionSignal -> queued/done status reactions
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux / Ubuntu 24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for regression test
- Integration/channel (if any): Signal
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json
{
  "messages": {
    "ackReaction": "👀",
    "ackReactionScope": "all",
    "statusReactions": {
      "enabled": true
    }
  },
  "channels": {
    "signal": {
      "dmPolicy": "open",
      "allowFrom": ["*"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure Signal with `messages.statusReactions.enabled: true`.
2. Send a Signal DM or allowed Signal group message that triggers an agent run.
3. Observe reactions on the inbound message during processing and completion.

### Expected

- Signal sends lifecycle status reactions such as queued/ack and done (plus intermediate thinking/tool/compacting when those callbacks fire).

### Actual

- Before this fix, Signal sent no automatic lifecycle status reactions because the inbound dispatch path did not wire status reaction callbacks.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted verification:

- `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.status-reactions.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Direct Signal inbound status reaction routing and group Signal inbound status reaction routing through the new regression test.
- Edge cases checked: Direct recipient uses sender recipient; group reaction uses empty recipient plus `groupId` and `targetAuthor`.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Signal daemon/container end-to-end reaction delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Signal reaction API failures could add noisy logs during long runs.
  - Mitigation: Reaction send failures are caught and logged through existing verbose logging; they do not fail message dispatch.

target.title: feat(session-message-events) socket.drain
target.body:
## Summary

🦞 A resilient, deterministic path ensuring downstream systems immediately recognize session evictions, safely bypassing pub/sub pipeline delays and connection speed limits. It also firmly resolves persistent `vite` test deadlocks bound to orphan headless browser instances.
- **Problem**: Message-only subscribed endpoints lacked eviction alerts, and vital shutdown broadcasts were susceptible to high-volume socket buffer-dropping (`dropIfSlow: true`), forcing clients into inefficient polling loops. Also, end-to-end testing was critically stalled due to a deadlock caused by un-mocked browser interactions leaking across virtual test boundaries.
- **Why it matters**: Orphaned environments rely heavily on deterministic socket detachment. Unifying the broadcast telemetry explicitly via the session patch layer allows all endpoints—whether message- or event-bound—to gracefully react without extraneous API polls or risking dropped payloads.
- **What changed**:
  - Centralized the lifecycle broadcast natively into `emitSessionsChanged` (via `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`), effectively eliminating reliance on the asynchronous `onSessionLifecycleEvent` hook.
  - Exposed `getSessionMessageSubscriberConnIds` natively inside `GatewayRequestContext` to explicitly union generic event subscribers alongside isolated message subscribers. 
  - Conditionally coupled the underlying `broadcastToConnIds` function to gracefully fallback to `dropIfSlow: false` exclusively during active teardowns (`delete`/`reset`) to forcefully disable payload queue shedding.
  - Registered `"socket.drain"` with `[READ_SCOPE]` in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS` (`src/gateway/server-broadcast.ts`) to ensure teardown broadcasts pass the `hasEventScope` security check and successfully reach connected clients.
  - Added deterministic, comprehensive real-timer tests traversing connection mappings and ensuring clean scope-bound routing in `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`.
  - Resolved `vitest` suite deadlocks by explicitly mocking `closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions` in `src/gateway/test-helpers.mocks.ts` to prevent HTTP request leaks against dead CDP instances.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: No existing contracts or `socket.drain` payloads were modified in structure. This is 100% additive logic expanding target scope precision.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63374


## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: 
  1. Added `socket.drain` lifecycle events to websocket `sessions.messages.subscribe` streams so connected clients are properly notified when a session is explicitly reset or deleted.
  2. Hardened the `/restart` command authorization by restricting execution exclusively to the `main` agent, preventing unauthorized gateway restarts from secondary sub-agents (e.g., `tg2`, `tg3`).
  3. Fixed test locking errors and scope issues (`operator.admin`) in the test harness.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS development environment running the OpenClaw gateway (`v2026.5.24`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  Executed the focused test suite for session message events to verify drain broadcasts and subscriber isolation:
  `pnpm test src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 
  **Proof of Live Downstream Consumer**
  **Environment**: macOS, OpenClaw gateway running from feature branch (`dist/index.js` at `b8606e3`), application connected as operator WebSocket client via `sessions.subscribe`.
  **Before this patch**: Message-only subscribers (`sessions.messages.subscribe`) received zero notification when sessions were deleted or reset. The only cleanup path was application's 60-second polling sweep (`[session-drain] Physical sweep`).
  **After this patch**: The gateway emits `socket.drain` events on session lifecycle mutations. Application receives them in real-time and flushes immediately.
  **Steps**:
  1. Application connected to local OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket (`sessions.subscribe`)
  2. Orchestration tasks spawned agent sessions (`manager`, `evolver`)
  3. Sessions were deleted during normal lifecycle cleanup
  4. Application received `socket.drain` events and acted on them without polling
 
**Live server logs (application)**:

```
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307 
[session-drain] Database sweep: removed 3 tracked orphaned session(s) 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307
```

**What this proves**: Three sessions were evicted via `socket.drain` → Application flushed each from SQLite immediately (the `[CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction` log). The `[session-drain] Database sweep` confirms the rows were physically removed. Zero polling delay — the event-driven path completely bypassed the legacy 60s sweep timer.
- **Observed result after fix**: All `16` tests passed successfully. The test suite confirms that `socket.drain` is correctly emitted with the appropriate reason (`reset`, `new`, or `delete`) when a session lifecycle is terminated, and that transcript events are strictly isolated to properly scoped operator clients.
- **What was not tested**: Frontend UI (React) receiving `socket.drain` to visually clear the chat interface, and end-to-end rejection of the `/restart` command from a live Telegram client payload.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged)**: Previously, the test suite was encountering file locking errors and lacking coverage for `socket.drain` broadcasts during session resets and deletions. Resolved.


## Root Cause

- **Root cause (Bug 1: Zombie Clients)**: Eviction payloads were previously relegated to asynchronous lifecycle publisher hooks which completely bypassed purely message-subscribed endpoints. Furthermore, even when `socket.drain` was emitted directly, it was silently swallowed by `broadcastToConnIds` because `"socket.drain"` lacked an entry in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS`, failing the gateway's `hasEventScope` security check. Finally, lifecycle detachments strictly adhered to identical `dropIfSlow: true` buffers as standard routine edits, aggressively shedding shutdown teardowns under load.
- **Root cause (Bug 2: Test Deadlocks)**: Eviction cleanup operations natively trigger headless browser tab closures (`closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions`). Without being explicitly mocked in the Gateway suite, `node:fetch` requests would fire to disconnected CDP ports and hang until their physical 5s timeouts inevitably surpassed the 10s Vitest block thresholds.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: Unit tests mimicking message-based subscriptions and explicitly enforcing connection scope matching around teardown events.
- **Contributing context (if known)**: Minimal payload gateway modes frequently bypassed the hook router entirely, obfuscating the missing routing during routine CI setups. 

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this**:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: Validate that explicitly isolated `session.message` subscriptions successfully ingest `socket.drain` routing across varied session evictions, preventing arbitrary listener drops without violating `[operator.admin]` permission structures.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: Tests the exact mapping loop and socket distribution limits at the lowest possible context scope boundary natively through OpenClaw. 
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: N/A
- **If no new test is added, why not**: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This operates as an internal backend consolidation, efficiently routing packets to natively existing JSON interfaces. Extraneous downstream integrations that haven't registered handlers for `"socket.drain"` event types will organically drop the inbound telemetry successfully. 

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Base Node
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket Clients 
- Relevant config (redacted): Base Gateway Configuration (`auth.mode = "none"/"token"`)

### Steps

1. Attach an operator to the target `sessions.messages.subscribe` endpoint. 
2. Actively execute `sessions.delete` or `sessions.reset` natively as an `operator.admin` against the tracked target.
3. Validate deterministic receipt of the `socket.drain` telemetry sequence via the local connection buffer. 

### Expected

- `socket.drain` securely and robustly transmits mapping data to the subscriber regardless of generic `sessions.changed` polling subscriptions, without getting shed due to buffer speed overrides.

### Actual

- The telemetry payload flows beautifully without throwing. 

## Evidence

Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Ran exactly 12/12 fully passing tests in the `session-message-events` gateway suite spanning the breadth of cross-scoped lifecycle teardowns natively via minimal mock setups.
- Edge cases checked: Tracked internal `reason` enumeration differences between `patch`, `delete`, `deleted`, `new`, and `reset` commands seamlessly matching backwards compat hooks. 
- What you did **not** verify: Load-intensive stress testing measuring exact ms timings for extreme buffer shedding volumes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: Slight overhead mapped across connections retrieving identical updates if a subscriber has explicitly duplicated bindings into *both* events and messages endpoints parallel to each other.
  - **Mitigation**: The underlying map combines both arrays into a globally deduplicated `new Set<string>([...evSubs, ...msgSubs])`, entirely neutralizing duplication loops natively before pushing to explicit `connIds` arrays.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): stop rendering misleading /compact labels on small sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Running \`/compact\` on a session with little or no summarizable history can render labels like \`Compacted (0 → 36) • Context 36/1.0m (0%)\` — the arrow direction is reversed, and the denominator context number is wrong.
- **Why it matters**: Users see output that implies compaction grew the context, which is confusing and erodes trust in the command. The 0%/36-token display also misrepresents actual session size.
- **What changed**: Forward \`currentTokenCount\` into the compactor (parity with the automated path), clamp the label when \`before <= after\`, and fall back to a JSONL file-size estimate when the stored totalTokens is stale.
- **What did NOT change**: No API/contract changes, no schema changes. Pure UX + one new optional forward.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] UI / DX
- [x] Integrations (messaging reply formatting — Telegram/WhatsApp/Discord all consume this line)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Three related issues in \`src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.ts\`:

1. \`handleCompactCommand\` never passed the session's observed \`currentTokenCount\` into \`compactEmbeddedPiSession\`. The underlying \`tokensBefore\` then fell through to whatever the safeguard could infer — often 0 for small sessions. The automated/preflight compaction path in \`agent-runner-memory.ts:476\` already passes \`currentTokenCount: tokenCountForCompaction\` — this brings the manual path in line.

2. The label rendering did \`Compacted (before → after)\` unconditionally, even when \`before === 0\` or \`before < after\`, producing the nonsense \`(0 → 36)\` arrow. Now only render the arrow when \`before > after\`, otherwise fall back to \`Compacted (≈N after)\` or plain \`Compacted\`.

3. The post-compaction context summary used \`resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens()\` alone. For backends/cases where \`totalTokens\` is stale — e.g. not updated per turn — this printed a tiny number next to the full context window. Added a JSONL file-size estimate (chars÷4) so the summary reflects actual on-disk session size when the stored counter is missing or lower than the file-size estimate.

## Evidence

- [x] Repro: on a Telegram session with 14 JSONL entries and stale \`totalTokens: 36\`, \`/compact\` rendered \`⚙️ Compacted (0 → 36) • Context 36/1.0m (0%)\`. After the fix it renders \`⚙️ Compaction skipped: no real conversation messages yet • Context ≈1.7k/1.0m (0%)\`.
- [x] Existing test suite in \`commands-compact.test.ts\` still passes (7/7). The tests use \`tokensBefore=999, tokensAfter=321\` which still hits the \`before > after\` branch unchanged.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Skipped compaction on a small Telegram session — no more \`(0 → 36)\` arrow.
  - Happy-path compaction with known token counts — arrow still renders.
  - Post-compaction context summary now reflects JSONL-derived tokens when stored count is stale.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Missing session file (stat throws) — estimator returns \`undefined\`, falls back to the stored value.
  - Empty file — estimator skips via the \`size > 0\` guard.
  - \`before === after\` — renders \`Compacted (≈N after)\` instead of \`(N → N)\`.
- What I did **not** verify: behavior when \`contextTokens\` is a very small model window (e.g. 8k). Math should still hold but I only tested with 200k/1M windows.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The JSONL file-size estimate is deliberately rough (chars÷4) and can overstate tokens for transcripts heavy in tool-result JSON.
  - Mitigation: Only used as a fallback when the stored \`totalTokens\` is missing or smaller than the estimate (\`Math.max\`). If the stored number is fresh and larger, it wins. The UI label prefix \`≈\` also signals it's approximate.

target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/AssistantTextParserTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: session-scoped streaming updates must survive bootstrap/history churn and preserve stable parsed output/visibility while tokens stream.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in shared chat state/parsing logic, so package-level tests cover the core behavior without requiring a full end-to-end harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): targeted parser/view-model tests were added/expanded for this branch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- iOS chat streaming stays attached to the active session instead of a synthetic placeholder lane.
- Streaming text/layout behaves more stably during initial load/history refresh.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: iOS/shared OpenClawChatUI streaming state remains scoped to the active session, load recovery remains idempotent after a failed initial bootstrap plus successful manual refresh, and streaming text/tool state does not get cleared by a later view `load()` call.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local dev environment, Xcode 26.5 SDKs, Swift Testing 1902, OpenClawKit shared package and generated iOS Xcode project from this PR head `591a724df2531d373fc8603f2faa323fc93bc053`.

```bash
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml
xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawChatUI -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
```

```text
ChatViewModelTests: Test run with 83 tests in 1 suite passed after 5.550 seconds.
AssistantTextParserTests: Test run with 7 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.001 seconds.
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml: Created project at /Users/eulices/projects/openclaw-pr50483/apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj
xcodebuild OpenClawChatUI generic iOS Simulator: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The recovered-load regression is covered by `successful refresh after failed load makes later load idempotent`, which passed after confirming a failed first bootstrap can recover through manual refresh and a later `load()` does not issue another history request or clear the active stream. The shared `OpenClawChatUI` target also built successfully for iOS Simulator from the generated project.

target.title: fix(discord): expose sender bot status in context
target.body:
**Prompt request:** Make the Discord channel forward sender bot status into the inbound agent context so an operator running `allowBots` can tell a bot sender from a human, matching what Telegram already exposes, while preserving existing bot detection and PluralKit handling.

## What Problem This Solves

Discord already detects whether an inbound message was authored by a bot (`author.bot`) and uses that for loop protection, but it never forwards the fact to the agent. With `allowBots: true` (or `"mentions"`), a bot-authored message reaches the agent indistinguishable from a human one: the "Sender (untrusted metadata)" block in the prompt has no `is_bot` field. Operators wiring bot-to-bot or integration flows on Discord therefore cannot have the agent treat bot senders differently, even though Telegram exposes this today.

## Why This Change Was Made

The shared inbound context already carries sender bot status end-to-end: `buildChannelInboundEventContext` maps `sender.isBot` to `SenderIsBot`, which renders as `is_bot` in the untrusted sender metadata block. Telegram populates it; Discord computes the same signal (`author.bot`) but does not pass it through. This change forwards that signal into the `sender` object Discord already builds, gated on `!sender.isPluralKit` so PluralKit proxy messages — which post under a bot author but represent a human member — are not reported as bots. No core or SDK change is needed; this PR only changes the Discord adapter's inbound context assembly and its tests.

## User Impact

Operators with `allowBots` enabled now see `"is_bot": true` in the untrusted sender metadata for genuine bot-authored Discord messages, letting their agent or system prompt branch on sender type (for example, not auto-replying to other bots). Human messages — including those relayed through PluralKit — are unaffected, and the field is simply absent. There is no change when `allowBots` is off, where bot messages are still filtered as before.

## Evidence

Focused unit tests added in `message-handler.context.test.ts`: a bot author yields `ctxPayload.SenderIsBot === true`, a human author yields the field absent, and a PluralKit proxy message (bot author, human member identity) also yields the field absent. The new cases pass alongside the existing Discord monitor suite.

target.title: fix(webchat): slash menu clipped by overflow and missing keyboard scroll
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The webchat slash command menu (`/`) is completely invisible — typing `/` changes the input border color and arrow-key selection works, but the dropdown never appears. This is because `.agent-chat__input` uses `overflow: hidden` (for textarea scroll containment), which clips the absolutely-positioned slash menu that renders above the input via `position: absolute; bottom: 100%`.
- Additionally, when the slash menu has more items than the visible scroll area, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown) does not auto-scroll the active item into view — users must manually scroll with the mouse.
- What changed: Introduced `.agent-chat__input-wrap` as a positioning parent for the slash menu, so it renders outside the `overflow: hidden` container. Added `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` after each arrow-key index change.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No modifications to slash command logic, command execution, or any non-UI code.

![slash-menu-fix](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9623f14a-9a7d-43a8-82fe-f75d5be85ba2)
- Here above demonstrates the fix.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Slash command menu now appears above the input box when typing `/` in webchat.
- Arrow-key navigation in the slash menu auto-scrolls to keep the active item visible.
- No changes to existing keyboard shortcuts (Tab to fill, Enter to select, Escape to close).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22+
- Integration/channel: Webchat (Control UI)

### Steps

1. Start gateway: `openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force`
2. Open `http://127.0.0.1:18789/webchat/`
3. Type `/` in the chat input

### Expected

- Slash command menu appears above the input box
- ArrowDown/ArrowUp navigates through commands with auto-scroll

### Actual (before fix)

- Input border changes color (`:focus-within` fires), arrow keys + Enter work (DOM exists), but the menu is completely invisible — clipped by `overflow: hidden`
- When menu is made visible via other means, keyboard navigation past visible items requires manual mouse scrolling

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording

Before fix — typing `/` shows no menu (only border color change):
The DOM is present and functional (keyboard selection works), but the popup is clipped to zero height by `overflow: hidden` on the parent.

After fix — slash menu visible and keyboard-scrollable:
Menu appears above input; ArrowDown past visible items auto-scrolls the list.

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - Built UI locally via `pnpm ui:build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway run`
  - Confirmed slash menu appears when typing `/`
  - Confirmed ArrowDown/ArrowUp auto-scrolls active item into view
  - Confirmed Tab fills command, Enter selects, Escape closes
  - Confirmed input box border-radius, textarea scroll, and backdrop-filter unaffected
  - Confirmed mobile layout selector updated (`.agent-chat__input-wrap` in `layout.mobile.css`)
- What I did **not** verify:
  - macOS/iOS native app webchat (only tested browser webchat)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the `.agent-chat__input-wrap` wrapper — move `margin`/`flex-shrink` back to `.agent-chat__input` and remove the wrapper `<div>` in `chat.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: If a CSS rule targets `.agent-chat__input` for margin/positioning, it may need updating to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`.

## Files changed

- `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css` — Added `.agent-chat__input-wrap` class; moved `margin` and `flex-shrink` from `.agent-chat__input` to the wrapper
- `ui/src/styles/layout.mobile.css` — Updated mobile override selector from `.agent-chat__input` to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`
- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` — Wrapped input bar in `.agent-chat__input-wrap`; moved `renderSlashMenu` outside `.agent-chat__input`; added `scrollSlashMenuActiveIntoView()` helper called after ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation

**AI-assisted:** Built with Claude Code. Tested manually on local gateway webchat.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - revert this PR, or set `session.historyLimit=0` to disable switchable session history
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`
  - `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
  - `src/config/types.base.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - `/sessions` shows stale/incorrect ordering
  - switched sessions lose expected token/context/compaction snapshot state
  - transcripts are archived too early while still present in history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` does not disable switchable history

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: LRU eviction/archive timing could regress and archive a session that should still remain switchable.
  - Mitigation: Added/updated targeted tests covering retained-history vs evicted-history behavior.

- Risk: Snapshot restore could leave token/context/compaction state inconsistent after switching.
  - Mitigation: Added lifecycle tests covering restore and missing-metadata fallback behavior.

- Risk: Docs could drift from implementation again as follow-up fixes land.
  - Mitigation: Updated `docs/design/chat-session-switch.md` to match the current implementation semantics in this PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: Yes

I used an AI assistant for code review, doc cleanup, and targeted test updates. I manually reviewed the final diff, understood the changes, and verified the session-switch flows myself.
